464	\section{The dimension-shift step}
465	
466	The induction driving the comparison theorem rests on a single short-exact-sequence
467	calculation: cutting a resolution into short exact sequences and reading off the
468	long exact sequence of right-derived functors. The latter is not available off the
469	shelf at the level of the derived-functor objects \((R^k G)\); we build it from the
470	complex-level homology long exact sequence
471	(Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) via the horseshoe lift
472	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}). We isolate the result as the kernel
473	sub-lemma below.
474	
475	\begin{lemma}[Dimension shift across an acyclic short exact sequence]
476	  \label{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}
477	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic}
478	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
479	    lem:injective_resolution_of_ses, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
480	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Derived Categories, Lemma
481	  % \texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses} (Tag 015D)
482	  % (read from references/homological-acyclic-derived.tex, L5619--5654).
483	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $F : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$ be a left exact functor
484	  % between abelian categories and assume
485	  % $RF : D^{+}(\mathcal{A}) \to D^{+}(\mathcal{B})$ is everywhere
486	  % defined. Let $0 \to A \to B \to C \to 0$ be a short exact sequence
487	  % of $\mathcal{A}$.
488	  % \begin{enumerate}
489	  % \item If $A$ and $C$ are right acyclic for $F$ then so is $B$.
490	  % \item If $A$ and $B$ are right acyclic for $F$ then so is $C$.
491	  % \item If $B$ and $C$ are right acyclic for $F$ and $F(B) \to F(C)$ is
492	  % surjective then $A$ is right acyclic for $F$.
493	  % \end{enumerate}
494	  % In each of the three cases
495	  % $$
496	  % 0 \to F(A) \to F(B) \to F(C) \to 0
497	  % $$
498	  % is a short exact sequence of $\mathcal{B}$."
499	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Derived Categories, Tag 015D
500	  (\texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses}).}
501	  Let \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be an additive left-exact functor, and let
502	  \[
503	    0 \longrightarrow A \longrightarrow J \longrightarrow Z \longrightarrow 0
504	  \]
505	  be a short exact sequence in \(\mathcal{A}\) with the middle term \(J\)
506	  right-\(G\)-acyclic. Then:
507	  \begin{enumerate}
508	    \item the connecting maps of the long exact sequence of right-derived functors
509	      furnish isomorphisms
510	      \[
511	        (R^{k} G)(Z) \;\xrightarrow{\ \cong\ }\; (R^{k+1} G)(A)
512	        \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1;
513	      \]
514	    \item in the lowest degree there is an isomorphism
515	      \[
516	        (R^{1} G)(A) \;\cong\; \operatorname{coker}\bigl(G(J) \to G(Z)\bigr),
517	      \]
518	      i.e.\ \((R^1 G)(A)\) is the cokernel of the map \(G(J) \to G(Z)\) induced by
519	      the surjection \(J \twoheadrightarrow Z\).
520	  \end{enumerate}
521	\end{lemma}
522	
523	% The Stacks proof of Tag 015D derives the long exact sequence from the
524	% delta-functor structure of $RF$ on the derived category $D^{+}(\mathcal{A})$.
525	% The proof below constructs the same sequence at the level of right-derived-functor
526	% objects from the complex-level homology long exact sequence
527	% (Lemma \ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) after a horseshoe lift
528	% (Lemma \ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}); accordingly no verbatim proof
529	% quote from Tag 015D is attached.
530	\begin{proof}
531	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
532	    lem:right_derived_injective_resolution, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence,
533	    lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}
534	  We first construct the long exact sequence of right-derived functors associated
535	  to \(0 \to A \to J \to Z \to 0\). In the classical treatment this sequence is
536	  supplied by the \(\delta\)-functor structure of \(\{R^k G\}\) on the derived
537	  category; here it is built directly from the complex-level homology long exact
538	  sequence via a horseshoe lift.
539	
540	  \emph{Building the long exact sequence.} By the horseshoe lift
541	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}) choose injective resolutions
542	  \(I_A, I_J, I_Z\) of \(A, J, Z\) fitting into a short exact sequence of cochain
543	  complexes
544	  \[
545	    0 \longrightarrow I_A \longrightarrow I_J \longrightarrow I_Z \longrightarrow 0
546	  \]
547	  that is degreewise split. Apply \(G\) degreewise. Because each degree
548	  \(0 \to I_A^n \to I_J^n \to I_Z^n \to 0\) is a \emph{split} short exact sequence
549	  (so \(I_J^n \cong I_A^n \oplus I_Z^n\) compatibly with the maps), the additive
550	  functor \(G\) carries it to a split short exact sequence
551	  \(0 \to G(I_A^n) \to G(I_J^n) \to G(I_Z^n) \to 0\) --- this is where degreewise
552	  splitness is essential, since \(G\) is not assumed exact and would not in general
553	  preserve a non-split short exact sequence. Hence
554	  \[
555	    0 \longrightarrow G(I_A) \longrightarrow G(I_J) \longrightarrow G(I_Z)
556	      \longrightarrow 0
557	  \]
558	  is a short exact sequence of cochain complexes in \(\mathcal{B}\). The
559	  complex-level homology long exact sequence
560	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) applied to it reads
561	  \[
562	    \cdots \to H^k\bigl(G(I_J)\bigr) \to H^k\bigl(G(I_Z)\bigr)
563	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } H^{k+1}\bigl(G(I_A)\bigr)
564	      \to H^{k+1}\bigl(G(I_J)\bigr) \to \cdots .
565	  \]
566	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution} the homology of each applied
567	  resolution computes the right-derived functor:
568	  \(H^k(G(I_A)) \cong (R^k G)(A)\), \(H^k(G(I_J)) \cong (R^k G)(J)\) and
569	  \(H^k(G(I_Z)) \cong (R^k G)(Z)\), naturally in the maps of the sequence. Transporting
570	  the homology long exact sequence across these isomorphisms gives the long exact
571	  sequence of right-derived functors
572	  \[
573	    \cdots \to (R^k G)(J) \to (R^k G)(Z)
574	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } (R^{k+1} G)(A) \to (R^{k+1} G)(J) \to \cdots,
575	  \]
576	  and, using \(R^0 G \cong G\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}) at the
577	  bottom, its low-degree initial segment
578	  \[
579	    0 \to G(A) \to G(J) \to G(Z)
580	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^0\ } (R^1 G)(A) \to (R^1 G)(J) \to \cdots .
581	  \]
582	
583	  \emph{Killing the acyclic terms.} Because \(J\) is right-\(G\)-acyclic
584	  (Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}), every term \((R^k G)(J)\) with \(k \geq 1\)
585	  vanishes. For \(k \geq 1\) the segment around \((R^k G)(J)\),
586	  \[
587	    \underbrace{(R^{k} G)(J)}_{=0} \to (R^{k} G)(Z)
588	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } (R^{k+1} G)(A) \to \underbrace{(R^{k+1} G)(J)}_{=0},
589	  \]
590	  has both outer terms zero, so the connecting map
591	  \(\delta^k : (R^{k} G)(Z) \to (R^{k+1} G)(A)\) is an isomorphism; this is part (1).
592	  In the lowest degree the segment
593	  \[
594	    G(J) \to G(Z) \xrightarrow{\ \delta^0\ } (R^1 G)(A)
595	      \to \underbrace{(R^1 G)(J)}_{=0}
596	  \]
597	  is exact with vanishing right end, so \(\delta^0\) is an epimorphism whose kernel is
598	  the image of \(G(J) \to G(Z)\); hence \(\delta^0\) identifies
599	  \(\operatorname{coker}\bigl(G(J) \to G(Z)\bigr)\) with \((R^1 G)(A)\), which is
600	  part (2).
601	\end{proof}
602	
603	\section{The acyclic-resolution comparison theorem}
604	
605	\begin{theorem}[An acyclic resolution computes the right-derived functor]
606	  \label{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
607	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution}
608	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
609	    lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
610	    lem:acyclic_dimension_shift, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence,
611	    lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}
612	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Derived Categories, Lemma
613	  % \texttt{lemma-leray-acyclicity} (Tag 015E, Leray's acyclicity lemma) and
614	  % Proposition \texttt{proposition-enough-acyclics} (Tag 05TA)
615	  % (read from references/homological-acyclic-derived.tex, L5692--5705 and
616	  % L5785--5811).
617	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $F : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$ be an additive functor
618	  % between abelian categories. Let $A^\bullet$ be a bounded below complex
619	  % of right $F$-acyclic objects such that $RF$ is defined at
620	  % $A^\bullet$. The canonical map
621	  % $$
622	  % F(A^\bullet) \longrightarrow RF(A^\bullet)
623	  % $$
624	  % is an isomorphism in $D^{+}(\mathcal{B})$, i.e., $A^\bullet$ computes
625	  % $RF$."
626	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $F : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$ be an additive functor of
627	  % abelian categories. ... If every object of $\mathcal{A}$ injects into an object
628	  % acyclic for $RF$, then $RF$ is defined on all of $K^{+}(\mathcal{A})$
629	  % and we obtain an exact functor
630	  % $$
631	  % RF : D^{+}(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow D^{+}(\mathcal{B})
632	  % $$
633	  % ... Moreover, any bounded below complex
634	  % $A^\bullet$ whose terms are acyclic for $RF$ computes $RF$."
635	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Derived Categories, Tag 015E (Leray's acyclicity
636	  lemma) and Tag 05TA (\texttt{proposition-enough-acyclics}).}
637	  Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be an abelian category with enough injectives, \(\mathcal{B}\)
638	  abelian, and \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) an additive left-exact functor.
639	  Let
640	  \[
641	    0 \longrightarrow A
642	      \longrightarrow J^0 \longrightarrow J^1 \longrightarrow J^2
643	      \longrightarrow \cdots
644	  \]
645	  be a resolution of \(A\) (an exact sequence) in which every object \(J^n\) is
646	  right-\(G\)-acyclic in the sense of Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}. Then for
647	  every \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) the \(n\)-th right-derived functor of \(G\) at \(A\) is
648	  computed by the resolution: there is an isomorphism in \(\mathcal{B}\)
649	  \[
650	    (R^n G)(A) \;\cong\; H^n\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr),
651	  \]
652	  where \(G(J^\bullet)\) is the cochain complex
653	  \(G(J^0) \to G(J^1) \to G(J^2) \to \cdots\) obtained by applying \(G\) degreewise
654	  to the augmentation-dropped resolution and \(H^n\) is its \(n\)-th cohomology
655	  object.
656	\end{theorem}
657	
658	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Say $I^n = 0$ for $n < n_0$. Setting $J^n = \Im(d^n)$ we break
659	% $I^\bullet$ into short exact sequences
660	% $0 \to J^n \to I^{n + 1} \to J^{n + 1} \to 0$
661	% for $n \geq n_0$. These sequences induce distinguished triangles
662	% $(J^n, I^{n + 1}, J^{n + 1})$ in $D^+(\mathcal{A})$ by
663	% Lemma \ref{lemma-derived-canonical-delta-functor}.
664	% ... By Proposition \ref{proposition-derived-functor} we have a
665	% distinguished triangle $(RF(J^n), RF(I^{n + 1}), RF(J^{n + 1}))$.
666	% Since $J^n, I^{n + 1}, J^{n + 1}$ are in $\mathcal{I}$
667	% the long exact cohomology sequence
668	% ... associated to this distinguished triangle collapses
669	% to an exact sequence
670	% $$
671	% 0 \to F(J^n) \to F(I^{n + 1}) \to F(J^{n + 1}) \to 0
672	% $$
673	% This in turn proves that $F(I^\bullet)$ is exact."
674	\begin{proof}
675	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
676	    lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
677	    lem:acyclic_dimension_shift, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence,
678	    lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}
679	  The argument is the classical dimension shift, with the long exact sequence at each
680	  step supplied by the kernel Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}. Decompose the
681	  resolution into the
682	  short exact sequences obtained from its cosyzygies. Put \(Z^0 := A\) and, for
683	  \(m \geq 1\), let \(Z^m := \ker\bigl(J^m \to J^{m+1}\bigr) =
684	  \operatorname{im}\bigl(J^{m-1} \to J^m\bigr)\) be the \(m\)-th cosyzygy; exactness
685	  of the resolution gives, for every \(m \geq 0\), a short exact sequence
686	  \[
687	    0 \longrightarrow Z^m \longrightarrow J^m \longrightarrow Z^{m+1}
688	      \longrightarrow 0, \tag{$S_m$}
689	  \]
690	  with \(Z^1 = \operatorname{im}(J^0 \to J^1) = \operatorname{coker}(A \to J^0)\) and
691	  the middle term \(J^m\) right-\(G\)-acyclic by hypothesis.
692	
693	  \emph{Base degrees.} Applying the left-exact functor \(G\) to the resolution and
694	  using Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self} (\(R^0 G \cong G\)), the complex
695	  \(G(J^\bullet)\) has, in degrees \(0\) and \(1\),
696	  \[
697	    H^0\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr) = \ker\bigl(G(J^0) \to G(J^1)\bigr) \cong G(A)
698	      = (R^0 G)(A),
699	  \]
700	  the last identification because \(G\) left exact carries
701	  \(0 \to A \to J^0 \to Z^1 \to 0\) to a left-exact sequence
702	  \(0 \to G(A) \to G(J^0) \to G(Z^1)\) and \(Z^1 \hookrightarrow J^1\) makes
703	  \(\ker(G(J^0) \to G(J^1)) = \ker(G(J^0) \to G(Z^1)) = G(A)\). For degree one,
704	  the dimension-shift Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}(2) applied to the
705	  sequence \((S_0)\), \(0 \to A \to J^0 \to Z^1 \to 0\), gives
706	  \[
707	    (R^1 G)(A) \;\cong\; \operatorname{coker}\bigl(G(J^0) \to G(Z^1)\bigr)
708	      \;\cong\; H^1\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr),
709	  \]
710	  the second isomorphism because \(G(J^0) \to G(Z^1) \hookrightarrow G(J^1)\) and the
711	  image of \(G(Z^1)\) in \(G(J^1)\) is exactly \(\ker(G(J^1) \to G(J^2))\) (again by
712	  left-exactness of \(G\) applied to \((S_1)\)), so the cokernel of
713	  \(G(J^0) \to G(Z^1)\) coincides with \(\ker(G(J^1) \to G(J^2)) /
714	  \operatorname{im}(G(J^0) \to G(J^1)) = H^1(G(J^\bullet))\).
715	
716	  \emph{Induction (the shift).} For \(n \geq 1\), iterate the connecting
717	  isomorphism. The sequences \((S_0), (S_1), \ldots, (S_{n-1})\) all have acyclic
718	  middle terms, so Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}(1) yields, for each
719	  \(1 \le m \le n-1\) and each \(k \geq 1\),
720	  \[
721	    (R^{k} G)(Z^{m+1}) \;\cong\; (R^{k+1} G)(Z^{m}),
722	  \]
723	  and applying part (1) to \((S_0)\) gives
724	  \((R^{k} G)(Z^{1}) \cong (R^{k+1} G)(A)\) for \(k \geq 1\). Composing these
725	  isomorphisms down the staircase
726	  \[
727	    (R^n G)(A)
728	      \;\cong\; (R^{n-1} G)(Z^1)
729	      \;\cong\; (R^{n-2} G)(Z^2)
730	      \;\cong\; \cdots
731	      \;\cong\; (R^{1} G)(Z^{n-1}),
732	  \]
733	  reduces the computation of \((R^n G)(A)\) to a single first-derived functor, which
734	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}(2) applied to \((S_{n-1})\),
735	  \(0 \to Z^{n-1} \to J^{n-1} \to Z^n \to 0\), evaluates as
736	  \[
737	    (R^1 G)(Z^{n-1}) \;\cong\; \operatorname{coker}\bigl(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)\bigr).
738	  \]
739	  Finally, as in the degree-one base case, left-exactness of \(G\) applied to
740	  \((S_n)\), \(0 \to Z^n \to J^n \to Z^{n+1} \to 0\), identifies the inclusion
741	  \(G(Z^n) \hookrightarrow G(J^n)\) with \(\ker(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1}))\), whence
742	  \[
743	    \operatorname{coker}\bigl(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)\bigr)
744	      \;\cong\;
745	      \frac{\ker\bigl(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1})\bigr)}
746	           {\operatorname{im}\bigl(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(J^n)\bigr)}
747	      \;=\; H^n\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr).
748	  \]
749	  Combining the staircase with this last identification gives
750	  \((R^n G)(A) \cong H^n(G(J^\bullet))\) for all \(n \geq 1\); together with the
751	  base degrees this proves the theorem for every \(n \in \mathbb{N}\).
752	
753	  Every long exact sequence consumed above is the one produced by the kernel
754	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}, and is therefore grounded --- through the
755	  horseshoe lift (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}) and the complex-level
756	  homology long exact sequence (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) --- on
757	  the injective-resolution computation of \(R^n G\)
758	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution}). The injective objects supplied
759	  by ``enough injectives'' are themselves right-\(G\)-acyclic
760	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective}), which is what makes an injective
761	  resolution a special case of an acyclic resolution and reconciles the present
762	  formula with the defining computation \((R^n G)(A) \cong H^n(G(I^\bullet))\) for an
763	  injective resolution \(I^\bullet\).
764	\end{proof}

1	# Analogy: SES → long exact sequence of right-derived functors (dimension shift)
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	p4-derived-les
8	
9	## Iteration
10	003
11	
12	## Question
13	To build "an acyclic resolution computes the right-derived functor" (Leray
14	acyclicity, Stacks 015E) at the level of `CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerived n`,
15	the kernel step is a dimension shift across a SES `0 → A → J → Z → 0` (J right-G-
16	acyclic) producing `(R^k G)(Z) ≅ (R^{k+1} G)(A)` (k ≥ 1) and
17	`(R^1 G)(A) ≅ coker(G J → G Z)`. This needs a SES → LES of right-derived functors.
18	Is the explicit horseshoe (`InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact`) the cheapest Mathlib-
19	aligned path, or does Mathlib already provide SES → LES of derived functors another
20	way (derived-category triangle, snake on a single resolution, or an Ext-level LES
21	that specializes to `rightDerived`)?
22	
23	## Project artifact(s)
24	- `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_AcyclicResolution.tex` — whole chapter;
25	  `lem:injective_resolution_of_ses` (horseshoe), `lem:acyclic_dimension_shift`
26	  (kernel), `lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived` (Leray 015E).
27	- Target Lean: `Functor.IsRightAcyclic`, `InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact`,
28	  `Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic`, `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`.
29	
30	## Decisions identified
31	
32	### Decision: how to obtain the SES → LES of `Functor.rightDerived n G`
33	
34	- **Mathlib idiom**: There is **no** SES → LES for `Functor.rightDerived n`. The
35	  closest analogues are NOT applicable:
36	  - *Ext LES* (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/ExactSequences.lean`,
37	    `covariantSequence` / `contravariantSequence`, `covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃`).
38	    These are built from the cohomological functors `preadditiveCoyoneda` /
39	    `preadditiveYoneda` applied to `triangleOfSES`'s homology sequence. They derive
40	    the LES of `Ext^n(X, –)` / `Ext^n(–, Y)` — i.e. of **derived Hom** — NOT of a
41	    general additive functor `G`. The project's `G = f_*` is not a Hom-functor, so
42	    this LES cannot be specialized to it. (`Ext.lean` defines `Ext` as
43	    `Abelian.Ext`, derived from `Hom` in the derived category — orthogonal to a
44	    user-supplied `G`.)
45	  - *Derived-category triangulated route* (`triangleOfSES` +
46	    `triangleOfSES_distinguished` in
47	    `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/ShortExact.lean`, plus
48	    `Pretriangulated.homologySequence_exact₁/₂/₃`). This gives the LES for a
49	    triangulated functor between derived categories. To feed `Functor.rightDerived n
50	    G` into it one needs the bridge
51	    `F.rightDerived n ≅ Hⁿ ∘ F.rightDerivedFunctorPlus ∘ Q` with
52	    `F.rightDerivedFunctorPlus : DerivedCategory.Plus C ⥤ DerivedCategory.Plus D`.
53	    **That bridge does not exist** — it is an explicit TODO in the module docstring
54	    of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Abelian/RightDerived.lean` (lines 40–47):
55	    "refactor `Functor.rightDerived` … when the necessary material enters mathlib:
56	    derived categories, injective/projective derivability structures, existence of
57	    derived functors from derivability structures. Eventually … redefined using
58	    `F.rightDerivedFunctorPlus`." Building it requires the injective derivability
59	    structure + existence-of-derived-functor machinery (the
60	    `CategoryTheory/Localization/DerivabilityStructure/*` + `Functor/Derived/*`
61	    framework) AND a comparison theorem — far more than the horseshoe.
62	- **Project's current path**: build `InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact` (dual
63	  Horseshoe Lemma) → apply G degreewise (split ⇒ preserved) → complex-level homology
64	  LES (`ShortComplex.ShortExact.δ/homology_exact₁/₂/₃`, present in
65	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/HomologySequence.lean:282–314`) → transport along
66	  `InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj` (present).
67	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL. The LES is genuinely absent for general `G`; the
68	  horseshoe is one self-contained way to manufacture it. The two "shortcut"
69	  alternatives are each strictly more expensive (Ext: wrong generality, unusable;
70	  derived-category: an explicitly-deferred Mathlib TODO).
71	- **Cost of divergence (if any)**: none — the horseshoe is the *cheaper* gap. The
72	  derived-category route would force the project to build (or wait for) the entire
73	  `rightDerivedFunctorPlus` bridge.
74	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (horseshoe is the right gap).
75	
76	### Decision: shape of `IsRightAcyclic`
77	
78	- **Mathlib idiom**: vanishing predicates over an object are `class … : Prop`
79	  (cf. `Injective`, `Projective` in
80	  `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Preadditive/Injective/Basic.lean`). The companion
81	  vanishing fact is `Functor.isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ` which states
82	  `IsZero ((F.rightDerived (n+1)).obj J)` for injective `J` — i.e. the
83	  index-shifted form `∀ k, IsZero ((R^{k+1} G) J)`.
84	- **Project's current path**: blueprint `def:right_acyclic` adopts exactly the
85	  index-shifted vanishing `(R^{k+1} G)(J) = 0 ∀ k`.
86	- **Gap**: identical (good). A `class IsRightAcyclic (G) (J) : Prop` wrapping
87	  `∀ k, IsZero ((G.rightDerived (k+1)).obj J)` mirrors Mathlib, and an `instance`
88	  deriving it for injectives from `isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ` is free.
89	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (align field shape with the `isZero …_succ` quantifier).
90	
91	## Recommendation
92	
93	Scaffold the **horseshoe** (`InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact`) — it is the right,
94	necessary, self-contained project-side gap, not a detour. Do NOT scaffold a derived-
95	category route: its prerequisite (`rightDerivedFunctorPlus` + injective derivability
96	structure) is an open Mathlib TODO and dwarfs the horseshoe. Do NOT try to specialize
97	the Ext LES: it is derived-Hom-specific and cannot see a general `G = f_*`.
98	
99	Concrete scaffold shape for the horseshoe and downstream:
100	1. `IsRightAcyclic G J : Prop` as a `class` = `∀ k, IsZero ((G.rightDerived (k+1)).obj J)`;
101	   instance for `[Injective J]` via `isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ`.
102	2. `InjectiveResolution.ofShortExact` — the hard part. Build `I_B` degreewise as
103	   `I_A.cocomplex.X n ⊞ I_C.cocomplex.X n` (biproduct ⇒ injective) with the
104	   **twisted** differential `[[d_A, τ], [0, d_C]]`; the off-diagonal `τ` and the
105	   augmentation `B → I_B^0` are produced stage-by-stage by the injective lifting
106	   property (`Injective.factorThru` against the relevant monos). Model the recursion
107	   on the existing `InjectiveResolution.ofCocomplex` / `exact_f_d` /
108	   `ofCocomplex_exactAt_succ` machinery
109	   (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Abelian/Injective/Resolution.lean:270–352`). Prove
110	   degreewise-split SES of complexes `0 → I_A → I_B → I_C → 0` and that `I_B`
111	   resolves `B` (exactness via the complex homology LES on the split SES).
112	   Expose the output as a `ShortComplex (CochainComplex C ℕ)` that `.ShortExact`.
113	3. `rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic` — apply `G` to the degreewise-split SES (additive
114	   `G` preserves split SES: use `ShortComplex.Splitting.map` /
115	   `Functor.mapShortComplex` on the per-degree splitting), get a `ShortExact` of
116	   `CochainComplex D ℕ`, feed `HomologySequence.δ` + `homology_exact₁/₂/₃`, transport
117	   each `Hⁿ(G(I_•))` to `(Rⁿ G)(•)` via `isoRightDerivedObj`, then kill the acyclic
118	   `(R^{≥1} G)(J)` terms.
119	
120	Feasibility note: the genuinely hard Lean sub-task is the twisted differential `τ`
121	and proving `I_B` is a resolution — a dependent recursion over `ℕ`. This is the
122	dominant risk and should get its own prover objective, scaffolded as a standalone
123	lemma chain (per-degree `τ`, chain-map laws, exactness) rather than one monolithic
124	definition. One cheap simplification to peel off first: the **injective-middle**
125	dimension shift (`0 → A → Q → A' → 0`, `Q` injective) needs NO horseshoe — it is a
126	re-indexing of a single injective resolution of `A` (shift `I_A^{≥1}`) and follows
127	from `isoRightDerivedObj` + `isZero_rightDerived_obj_injective_succ`. It does NOT
128	discharge the acyclic-middle case the cosyzygy SESs require, so it cannot replace the
129	horseshoe — but it is a useful warm-up / sanity lemma and may shorten the base degree.
130
This is a substantial task: building the dual Horseshoe Lemma in Lean. Let me first verify the current state and study the Mathlib infrastructure.

namespace Lean.Elab.Command

@[builtin_command_elab moduleDoc] def elabModuleDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let some range ← Elab.getDeclarationRange? stx
    | return  -- must be from partial syntax, ignore

  match stx[1] with
  | Syntax.atom _ val =>
    if getMainVersoModuleDocs (← getEnv) |>.isEmpty then
      let doc := String.Pos.Raw.extract val 0 (val.rawEndPos.unoffsetBy ⟨2⟩)
      modifyEnv fun env => addMainModuleDoc env ⟨doc, range⟩
    else
      throwError m!"Can't add Markdown-format module docs because there is already Verso-format content present."
  | Syntax.node _ ``Lean.Parser.Command.versoCommentBody args =>
    let docSyntax := args.getD 0 .missing
    if docSyntax.getKind == `Lean.Doc.Syntax.parseFailure then
      -- Report parser errors without attempting elaboration
      runTermElabM fun _ => reportVersoParseFailure docSyntax
    else
      runTermElabM fun _ => do
        addVersoModDocString range ⟨docSyntax⟩
  | _ => throwErrorAt stx "unexpected module doc string{indentD <| stx}"

private def addScope (isNewNamespace : Bool) (header : String) (newNamespace : Name)
    (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false) (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) :
    CommandElabM Unit := do
  modify fun s => { s with
    env    := s.env.registerNamespace newNamespace,
    scopes := { s.scopes.head! with
      header := header, currNamespace := newNamespace
      isNoncomputable := s.scopes.head!.isNoncomputable || isNoncomputable
      isPublic := s.scopes.head!.isPublic || isPublic
      isMeta := s.scopes.head!.isMeta || isMeta
      attrs := s.scopes.head!.attrs ++ attrs
    } :: s.scopes
  }
  pushScope
  if isNewNamespace then
    activateScoped newNamespace

private def addScopes (header : Name) (isNewNamespace : Bool) (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false)
    (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  go header
where go
  | .anonymous => pure ()
  | .str p header => do
    go p
    let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
    addScope isNewNamespace header (if isNewNamespace then Name.mkStr currNamespace header else currNamespace) isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta attrs
  | _ => throwError "invalid scope"

private def addNamespace (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  addScopes (isNewNamespace := true) (isNoncomputable := false) (attrs := []) header

private def popScopes (numScopes : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  for _ in *...numScopes do
    popScope

def withNamespace {α} (ns : Name) (elabFn : CommandElabM α) : CommandElabM α := do
  addNamespace ns
  let a ← elabFn
  modify fun s => { s with scopes := s.scopes.drop ns.getNumParts }
  popScopes ns.getNumParts
  pure a

private def innermostScopeName? : List Scope → Option Name
  | { header := "", .. } :: _ => none
  | { header := h, .. }  :: _ => some <| .mkSimple h
  | _                         => some .anonymous -- should not happen

private def checkEndHeader : Name → List Scope → Option Name
  | .anonymous, _ => none
  | .str p s, { header := h, .. } :: scopes =>
    if h == s then
      (.str · s) <$> checkEndHeader p scopes
    else
      some <| .mkSimple h
  | _, _ => some .anonymous -- should not happen

@[builtin_command_elab «namespace»] def elabNamespace : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  match stx with
  | `(namespace $n) => addNamespace n.getId
  | _               => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «section»] def elabSection : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `(Parser.Command.section| $[@[expose%$expTk]]? $[public%$publicTk]? $[noncomputable%$ncTk]? $[meta%$metaTk]? section $(header?)?) =>
    -- TODO: allow more attributes?
    let attrs ← if expTk.isSome then
      pure [← `(Parser.Term.attrInstance| expose)]
    else
      pure []
    if let some header := header? then
      addScopes (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) header.getId
    else
      addScope (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) "" (← getCurrNamespace)
  | _                        => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab InternalSyntax.end_local_scope] def elabEndLocalScope : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  setDelimitsLocal

/--
Produces a `Name` composed of the names of at most the innermost `n` scopes in `ss`, truncating if an
empty scope is reached (so that we do not suggest names like `Foo.«».Bar`).

If `n` is not specified, will use all scopes in `ss`.
-/
private def nameOfScopes : (ss : List Scope) → (n : Nat := ss.length) → Name
  | _, 0 => .anonymous
  | [], _ => .anonymous
  | s :: ss, n + 1 =>
    if s.header == "" then
      .anonymous
    else
    .str (nameOfScopes ss n) s.header

/--
Returns the first suffix of `base` that begins with `seg`, if one exists.

Note: this uses a naive, O(m*n) implementation for simplicity; we assume repeated partial overlap of
name components to be relatively uncommon, so that practical performance is closer to linear.
-/
private def findSuffixWithPrefix (base : Name) (seg : Name) : Option Name :=
  findSuffixMatch base seg true
where
  /--
  Helper for `findSuffixWithPrefix`. If `allowOffset` is `false`, then `seg` must be a suffix of
  `base`, not just a prefix of a suffix.
  -/
  findSuffixMatch : (base : Name) → (seg : Name) → (allowOffset : Bool) → Option Name
  | _, .anonymous, _ => some .anonymous
  | .anonymous, _, _ => none
  | .num p n, seg@(.num p' n'), allowOffset => do
    if n == n' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .num nm n
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg@(.str p' s'), allowOffset => do
    if s == s' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .str nm s
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .num p n, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none

private def throwNoScope : CommandElabM Unit :=
  throwError m!"Invalid `end`: There is no current scope to end"
    ++ .note m!"Scopes are introduced using `namespace` and `section`"

private def throwMissingName (name : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint m!"To end the current scope `{name}`, specify its name:"
    #[← `(end $(mkIdent name))] (codeActionPrefix? := "Add scope name: ")
  throwError "Missing name after `end`: Expected the current scope name `{name}`{hint}"

/--
Produces a hint message with a suggestion to replace the name following `end` at the current ref
with the name given by `scopes` if there is only one active scope; otherwise, returns `none`.

Rationale: When there is only one active scope, only one valid `end` command is possible, so we
suggest it; if there are multiple, then it is harder to determine with confidence which the user
intended to end.
-/
private def mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? (scopes : List Scope) := do
  -- Recall that there is always an anonymous topmost scope, so `scopes.length = 2` when there is
  -- only one active scope:
  if scopes.length == 2 then
    let name := nameOfScopes scopes
    some <$> MessageData.hint m!"Use current scope name `{name}`:" #[(← `(end $(mkIdent name)))]
      (codeActionPrefix? := "Replace scope name: ")
  else
    return none

private def throwTooManyScopeComponents (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes
      pure <| MessageData.hint' m!"The name after `end` must be `{scopesName}` or some suffix thereof"
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: `{header}` contains too many components" ++ hint

private def throwScopeNameMismatch (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) (endSize : Nat)
    : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let correspondingScopes := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
  let allScopes := nameOfScopes scopes
  let help ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else if let some suffix := findSuffixWithPrefix allScopes header then
      let hintMsg := m!"If you meant to end the outer scope(s) `{header}`, you must end all the \
        intervening scopes `{suffix}`:"
      MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end $(mkIdent suffix))]
        (codeActionPrefix? := "Add intervening scopes: ")
    else if correspondingScopes != allScopes then
      pure <| .note m!"The current scopes are `{allScopes}`"
    else pure .nil
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: Expected `{correspondingScopes}`, but found `{header}`" ++ help

private def throwUnnecessaryScopeName (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hintMsg := m!"Delete the name `{header}` to end the current unnamed scope; outer named scopes \
    can then be closed using additional `end` command(s):"
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end)] (codeActionPrefix? := "Delete name: ")
  throwError m!"Unexpected name `{header}` after `end`: The current section is unnamed" ++ hint

@[builtin_command_elab «end»] def elabEnd : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(end $[$header? $[.%$trailingDotTk?$_]?]?) := stx
    | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let header? := header?.map (·.getId)
  let danglingDot := trailingDotTk?.join.isSome
  let endSize : Nat := match header? with
    | none   => 1
    | some n => n.getNumParts
  let scopes ← getScopes
  let numScopes := scopes.length
  if numScopes == 1 then
    throwNoScope
  match header? with
  | none        =>
    addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx none false <| scopes.map (·.header)
    if let some name := innermostScopeName? scopes then
      throwMissingName name
  | some header =>
    if endSize >= numScopes then
      throwTooManyScopeComponents header scopes
    else
      addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx header danglingDot <| scopes.map (·.header)
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
      if scopesName != header then
        if scopesName == .anonymous then
          throwUnnecessaryScopeName header
        else
          throwScopeNameMismatch header scopes endSize
  modify fun s => {s with scopes := s.scopes.drop endSize }
  popScopes endSize

private partial def elabChoiceAux (cmds : Array Syntax) (i : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  if h : i < cmds.size then
    catchInternalId unsupportedSyntaxExceptionId
      (elabCommand cmds[i])
      (fun _ => elabChoiceAux cmds (i+1))
  else
    throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab choice] def elabChoice : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  elabChoiceAux stx.getArgs 0

@[builtin_command_elab «universe»] def elabUniverse : CommandElab := fun n => do
  n[1].forArgsM addUnivLevel

@[builtin_command_elab «init_quot»] def elabInitQuot : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  liftCoreM <| addDecl Declaration.quotDecl

@[builtin_command_elab «docs_to_verso»] def elabDocsToVerso : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let xs := stx[1].getArgs
  for x in xs do
    if x.getKind == identKind then -- skip commas
      let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverload x
      runTermElabM <| fun _ => withRef x <| makeDocStringVerso declName
      -- Add the info afterwards so the hover shows the updated docstring
      addConstInfo x declName

@[builtin_command_elab «export»] def elabExport : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(export $ns ($ids*)) := stx | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let nss ← resolveNamespace ns
  let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
  if nss == [currNamespace] then throwError "invalid 'export', self export"
  let mut aliases := #[]
  for idStx in ids do
    let id := idStx.getId
    let declName ← resolveNameUsingNamespaces nss idStx
    if (← getInfoState).enabled then
      addConstInfo idStx declName
    aliases := aliases.push (currNamespace ++ id, declName)
  modify fun s => { s with env := aliases.foldl (init := s.env) fun env p => addAlias env p.1 p.2 }

@[builtin_command_elab «open»] def elabOpen : CommandElab
  | `(open $decl:openDecl) => do
    let openDecls ← elabOpenDecl decl
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with openDecls := openDecls }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Lean.Parser.Term

private def typelessBinder? : Syntax → Option (Array (TSyntax [`ident, `Lean.Parser.Term.hole]) × BinderInfo)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids*))     => some (ids, .default)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids*})     => some (ids, .implicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids*⦄)     => some (ids, .strictImplicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id:ident]) => some (#[id], .instImplicit)
  | _                               => none

/--  If `id` is an identifier, return true if `ids` contains `id`. -/
private def containsId (ids : Array (TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole])) (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) : Bool :=
  id.raw.isIdent && ids.any fun id' => id'.raw.getId == id.raw.getId

/--
  Auxiliary method for processing binder annotation update commands:
  `variable (α)`, `variable {α}`, `variable ⦃α⦄`, and `variable [α]`.
  The argument `binder` is the binder of the `variable` command.
  The method returns an array containing the "residue", that is, variables that do not correspond to updates.
  Recall that a `bracketedBinder` can be of the form `(x y)`.
  ```
  variable {α β : Type}
  variable (α γ)
  ```
  The second `variable` command updates the binder annotation for `α`, and returns "residue" `γ`.
-/
private def replaceBinderAnnotation (binder : TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) : CommandElabM (Array (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder)) := do
  let some (binderIds, binderInfo) := typelessBinder? binder | return #[binder]
  let varDecls := (← getScope).varDecls
  let mut varDeclsNew := #[]
  let mut binderIds := binderIds
  let mut binderIdsIniSize := binderIds.size
  let mut modifiedVarDecls := false
  -- Go through declarations in reverse to respect shadowing
  for varDecl in varDecls.reverse do
    let (ids, ty?, binderInfo') ← match varDecl with
      | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids* $[: $ty?]? $(annot?)?)) =>
        if annot?.isSome then
          for binderId in binderIds do
            if containsId ids binderId then
              throwErrorAt binderId "cannot update binder annotation of variables with default values/tactics"
        pure (ids, ty?, .default)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids* $[: $ty?]?}) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .implicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids* $[: $ty?]?⦄) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .strictImplicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id : $ty]) =>
        pure (#[⟨id⟩], some ty, .instImplicit)
      | _ =>
        varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl; continue
    if binderInfo == binderInfo' then
      -- no update, ensure we don't have redundant annotations.
      for binderId in binderIds do
        if containsId ids binderId then
          throwErrorAt binderId "redundant binder annotation update"
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else if binderIds.all fun binderId => !containsId ids binderId then
      -- `binderIds` and `ids` are disjoint
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else
      let mkBinder (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) (binderInfo : BinderInfo) : CommandElabM (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) :=
        match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($id $[: $ty?]?))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$id $[: $ty?]?})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$id $[: $ty?]?}})
        | .instImplicit => do
          let some ty := ty?
            | throwErrorAt binder "cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                variable was originally declared without an explicit type"
          `(bracketedBinderF| [$(⟨id⟩) : $ty])
      for id in ids.reverse do
        if let some idx := binderIds.findFinIdx? fun binderId => binderId.raw.isIdent && binderId.raw.getId == id.raw.getId then
          binderIds := binderIds.eraseIdx idx
          modifiedVarDecls := true
          let newBinder ← mkBinder id binderInfo
          if binderInfo.isInstImplicit then
            -- We elaborate the new binder to make sure it's valid as instance implicit
            try
              runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
                Term.elabBinder newBinder fun _ => pure ()
            catch e =>
              throwErrorAt binder m!"cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                {e.toMessageData}"
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo)
        else
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo')
  if modifiedVarDecls then
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := varDeclsNew.reverse }
  if binderIds.size != binderIdsIniSize then
    binderIds.mapM fun binderId =>
      match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($binderId))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$binderId})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$binderId}})
        | .instImplicit => throwUnsupportedSyntax
  else
    return #[binder]

@[builtin_command_elab «variable»] def elabVariable : CommandElab
  | `(variable%$tk $binders*) => do
    let binders ← binders.flatMapM replaceBinderAnnotation
    -- Try to elaborate `binders` for sanity checking
    runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
      Term.elabBinders binders fun xs => do
        -- Determine the set of auto-implicits for this variable command and add an inlay hint
        -- for them. We will only actually add the auto-implicits to a type when the variables
        -- declared here are used in some other declaration, but this is nonetheless the right
        -- place to display the inlay hint.
        let _ ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (tk.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
    -- Remark: if we want to produce error messages when variables shadow existing ones, here is the place to do it.
    for binder in binders do
      let varUIds ← (← getBracketedBinderIds binder) |>.mapM (withFreshMacroScope ∘ MonadQuotation.addMacroScope)
      modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := scope.varDecls.push binder, varUIds := scope.varUIds ++ varUIds }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Meta

def elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC : Bool) : CommandElab
  | `(#check%$tk $term) => withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_check do
    -- show signature for `#check id`/`#check @id`
    if let `($id:ident) := term then
      try
        for c in (← realizeGlobalConstWithInfos term) do
          addCompletionInfo <| .id term id.getId (danglingDot := false) {} none
          logInfoAt tk <| .signature c
          return
      catch _ => pure ()  -- identifier might not be a constant but constant + projection
    let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing (ignoreStuckTC := ignoreStuckTC)
    -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
    withRef tk <| Meta.check e
    let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
    if e.isSyntheticSorry then
      return
    let type ← inferType e
    logInfoAt tk m!"{e} : {type}"
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.check] def elabCheck : CommandElab := elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := true)

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.reduceCmd] def elabReduce : CommandElab
  | `(#reduce%$tk $term) => go tk term
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipTypes := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false) (skipTypes := false)
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
where
  go (tk : Syntax) (term : Syntax) (skipProofs := true) (skipTypes := true) : CommandElabM Unit :=
    withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_reduce do
      let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
      withRef tk <| Meta.check e
      let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
      -- TODO: add options or notation for setting the following parameters
      withTheReader Core.Context (fun ctx => { ctx with options := ctx.options.set `smartUnfolding false }) do
        let e ← withTransparency (mode := TransparencyMode.all) <| reduce e (skipProofs := skipProofs) (skipTypes := skipTypes)
        logInfoAt tk e

def hasNoErrorMessages : CommandElabM Bool := do
  return !(← get).messages.hasErrors

def failIfSucceeds (x : CommandElabM Unit) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let resetMessages : CommandElabM MessageLog := do
    let s ← get
    let messages := s.messages;
    modify fun s => { s with messages := {} };
    pure messages
  let restoreMessages (prevMessages : MessageLog) : CommandElabM Unit := do
    modify fun s => { s with messages := prevMessages ++ s.messages.errorsToInfos }
  let prevMessages ← resetMessages
  let succeeded ← try
    x
    hasNoErrorMessages
  catch
    | ex@(Exception.error _ _) => do logException ex; pure false
    | Exception.internal id _  => do logError (← id.getName); pure false
  finally
    restoreMessages prevMessages
  if succeeded then
    throwError "unexpected success"

@[builtin_command_elab «check_failure»] def elabCheckFailure : CommandElab
  | `(#check_failure $term) => do
    failIfSucceeds <| elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := false) (← `(#check $term))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «synth»] def elabSynth : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let term := stx[1]
  withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_synth_cmd do
    let inst ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
    let inst ← instantiateMVars inst
    let val  ← synthInstance inst
    logInfo val
    pure ()

@[builtin_command_elab «set_option»] def elabSetOption : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let options ← Elab.elabSetOption stx[1] stx[3]
  modify fun s => { s with maxRecDepth := maxRecDepth.get options }
  modifyScope fun scope => { scope with opts := options }

open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.«in»] def expandInCmd : Macro
  | `($cmd₁ in%$tk $cmd₂) =>
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    withRef tk `(section $cmd₁:command $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $cmd₂ end)
  | _                 => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.addDocString] def elabAddDeclDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `($doc:docComment add_decl_doc $id) =>
    let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverloadWithInfo id
    unless ((← getEnv).getModuleIdxFor? declName).isNone do
      throwError "invalid 'add_decl_doc', declaration is in an imported module"
    if let .none ← findDeclarationRangesCore? declName then
      -- this is only relevant for declarations added without a declaration range
      -- in particular `Quot.mk` et al which are added by `init_quot`
      addDeclarationRangesFromSyntax declName stx id
    runTermElabM fun _ => addDocString declName (mkNullNode #[]) doc
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.include] def elabInclude : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.include| include $ids*) => do
    let sc ← getScope
    let vars ← sc.varDecls.flatMapM getBracketedBinderIds
    let mut uids := #[]
    for id in ids do
      if let some idx := vars.findIdx? (· == id.getId) then
        uids := uids.push sc.varUIds[idx]!
      else
        throwError "invalid 'include', variable `{id}` has not been declared in the current scope"
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      includedVars := sc.includedVars ++ uids.toList
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars.filter (!uids.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.omit] def elabOmit : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.omit| omit $omits*) => do
    -- TODO: this really shouldn't have to re-elaborate section vars... they should come
    -- pre-elaborated
    let omittedVars ← runTermElabM fun vars => do
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- We don't want to store messages produced when elaborating `(getVarDecls s)` because they have already been saved when we elaborated the `variable`(s) command.
      -- So, we use `Core.resetMessageLog`.
      Core.resetMessageLog
      -- resolve each omit to variable user name or type pattern
      let elaboratedOmits : Array (Sum Name Expr) ← omits.mapM fun
        | `(ident| $id:ident) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$id : $_]) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$ty]) =>
          Sum.inr <$> Term.withoutErrToSorry (Term.elabTermAndSynthesize ty none)
        | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
      -- check that each omit is actually used in the end
      let mut omitsUsed := omits.map fun _ => false
      let mut omittedVars := #[]
      let mut revSectionFVars : Std.HashMap FVarId Name := {}
      for (uid, var) in (← read).sectionFVars do
        revSectionFVars := revSectionFVars.insert var.fvarId! uid
      for var in vars do
        let ldecl ← var.fvarId!.getDecl
        if let some idx := (← elaboratedOmits.findIdxM? fun
            | .inl id => return ldecl.userName == id
            | .inr ty => do
              let mctx ← getMCtx
              isDefEq ty ldecl.type <* setMCtx mctx) then
          if let some uid := revSectionFVars[var.fvarId!]? then
            omittedVars := omittedVars.push uid
            omitsUsed := omitsUsed.set! idx true
          else
            throwError "invalid 'omit', `{ldecl.userName}` has not been declared in the current scope"
      for o in omits, used in omitsUsed do
        unless used do
          throwError "`{o}` did not match any variables in the current scope"
      return omittedVars
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars ++ omittedVars.toList
      includedVars := sc.includedVars.filter (!omittedVars.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab version] def elabVersion : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let mut target := System.Platform.target
  if target.isEmpty then target := "unknown"
  -- Only one should be set, but good to know if multiple are set in error.
  let platforms :=
    (if System.Platform.isWindows then [" Windows"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isOSX then [" macOS"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isEmscripten then [" Emscripten"] else [])
  logInfo m!"Lean {Lean.versionString}\nTarget: {target}{String.join platforms}"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.exit] def elabExit : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  logWarning "using 'exit' to interrupt Lean"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.import] def elabImport : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  throwError "invalid 'import' command, it must be used in the beginning of the file"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.eoi] def elabEoi : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  return

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.where] def elabWhere : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let scope ← getScope
  let mut msg : Array MessageData := #[]
  -- Noncomputable
  if scope.isNoncomputable then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(Parser.Command.section| noncomputable section)
  -- Namespace
  if !scope.currNamespace.isAnonymous then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| namespace $(mkIdent scope.currNamespace))
  -- Open namespaces
  if let some openMsg ← describeOpenDecls scope.openDecls.reverse then
    msg := msg.push openMsg
  -- Universe levels
  if !scope.levelNames.isEmpty then
    let levels := scope.levelNames.reverse.map mkIdent
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| universe $levels.toArray*)
  -- Variables
  if !scope.varDecls.isEmpty then
    let varDecls : Array (TSyntax `Lean.Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) := scope.varDecls.map (⟨·.raw.unsetTrailing⟩)
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| variable $varDecls*)
  -- Included variables
  if !scope.includedVars.isEmpty then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| include $(scope.includedVars.toArray.map (mkIdent ·.eraseMacroScopes))*)
  -- Options
  if let some optionsMsg ← describeOptions scope.opts then
    msg := msg.push optionsMsg
  if msg.isEmpty then
    logInfo m!"-- In root namespace with initial scope"
  else
    logInfo <| MessageData.joinSep msg.toList "\n\n"
where
  /--
  'Delaborate' open declarations.
  Current limitations:
  - does not check whether or not successive namespaces need `_root_`
  - does not combine commands with `renaming` clauses into a single command
  -/
  describeOpenDecls (ds : List OpenDecl) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let mut simple : Array Name := #[]
    let flush (lines : Array MessageData) (simple : Array Name) : CommandElabM (Array MessageData × Array Name) := do
      if simple.isEmpty then
        return (lines, simple)
      else
        return (lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(simple.map mkIdent)*), #[])
    for d in ds do
      match d with
      | .explicit id decl =>
        (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
        let ns := decl.getPrefix
        let «from» := Name.mkSimple decl.getString!
        lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) renaming $(mkIdent «from») → $(mkIdent id))
      | .simple ns ex =>
        if ex == [] then
          simple := simple.push ns
        else
          (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
          lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) hiding $[$(ex.toArray.map mkIdent)]*)
    (lines, _) ← flush lines simple
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

  describeOptions (opts : Options) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let decls ← getOptionDecls
    for (name, val) in opts do
      -- `#guard_msgs` sets this option internally, we don't want it to end up in its output
      if name == `Elab.async then
        continue
      let (isSet, isUnknown) :=
        match decls.find? name with
        | some decl => (decl.defValue != val, false)
        | none      => (true, true)
      if isSet then
        let cmd : TSyntax `command ←
          match val with
          | .ofBool true  => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) true)
          | .ofBool false => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) false)
          | .ofString str => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkStrLit str))
          | .ofNat n      => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkNatLit n))
          | _             => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) 0 /- unrepresentable value -/)
        if isUnknown then
          lines := lines.push m!"-- {cmd} -- unknown option"
        else
          lines := lines.push cmd
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.withExporting] def elabWithExporting : CommandElab
  | `(Parser.Command.withExporting| #with_exporting $cmd) =>
    withExporting do
      elabCommand cmd
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.dumpAsyncEnvState] def elabDumpAsyncEnvState : CommandElab :=
  fun _ => do
    let env ← getEnv
    IO.eprintln (← env.dbgFormatAsyncState)

end Lean.Elab.Command

15	/-!
16	# Abelian categories with enough injectives have injective resolutions
17	
18	## Main results
19	When the underlying category is abelian:
20	* `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.desc`: Given `I : InjectiveResolution X` and
21	  `J : InjectiveResolution Y`, any morphism `X ⟶ Y` admits a descent to a cochain map
22	  `J.cocomplex ⟶ I.cocomplex`. It is a descent in the sense that `I.ι` intertwines the descent and
23	  the original morphism, see `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.desc_commutes`.
24	* `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.descHomotopy`: Any two such descents are homotopic.
25	* `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.homotopyEquiv`: Any two injective resolutions of the same
26	  object are homotopy equivalent.
27	* `CategoryTheory.injectiveResolutions`: If every object admits an injective resolution, we can
28	  construct a functor `injectiveResolutions C : C ⥤ HomotopyCategory C`.
29	
30	* `CategoryTheory.exact_f_d`: `f` and `Injective.d f` are exact.
31	* `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution.of`: Hence, starting from a monomorphism `X ⟶ J`, where `J`
32	  is injective, we can apply `Injective.d` repeatedly to obtain an injective resolution of `X`.
33	-/
34

36	
37	noncomputable section

56	/-- Auxiliary construction for `desc`. -/
57	def descFZero {Y Z : C} (f : Z ⟶ Y) (I : InjectiveResolution Y) (J : InjectiveResolution Z) :
58	    J.cocomplex.X 0 ⟶ I.cocomplex.X 0 :=
59	  factorThru (f ≫ I.ι.f 0) (J.ι.f 0)

67	lemma exact₀ {Z : C} (I : InjectiveResolution Z) :
68	    (ShortComplex.mk _ _ I.ι_f_zero_comp_complex_d).Exact :=
69	  ShortComplex.exact_of_f_is_kernel _ I.isLimitKernelFork

71	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
72	/-- Auxiliary construction for `desc`. -/
73	def descFOne {Y Z : C} (f : Z ⟶ Y) (I : InjectiveResolution Y) (J : InjectiveResolution Z) :
74	    J.cocomplex.X 1 ⟶ I.cocomplex.X 1 :=
75	  J.exact₀.descToInjective (descFZero f I J ≫ I.cocomplex.d 0 1)
76	    (by dsimp; simp only [← assoc, descFZero]; simp [assoc])
77	
78	@[simp]
79	theorem descFOne_zero_comm {Y Z : C} (f : Z ⟶ Y) (I : InjectiveResolution Y)
80	    (J : InjectiveResolution Z) :
81	    J.cocomplex.d 0 1 ≫ descFOne f I J = descFZero f I J ≫ I.cocomplex.d 0 1 := by
82	  apply J.exact₀.comp_descToInjective
83	
84	/-- Auxiliary construction for `desc`. -/
85	def descFSucc {Y Z : C} (I : InjectiveResolution Y) (J : InjectiveResolution Z) (n : ℕ)
86	    (g : J.cocomplex.X n ⟶ I.cocomplex.X n) (g' : J.cocomplex.X (n + 1) ⟶ I.cocomplex.X (n + 1))
87	    (w : J.cocomplex.d n (n + 1) ≫ g' = g ≫ I.cocomplex.d n (n + 1)) :
88	    Σ' g'' : J.cocomplex.X (n + 2) ⟶ I.cocomplex.X (n + 2),
89	      J.cocomplex.d (n + 1) (n + 2) ≫ g'' = g' ≫ I.cocomplex.d (n + 1) (n + 2) :=
90	  ⟨(J.exact_succ n).descToInjective
91	    (g' ≫ I.cocomplex.d (n + 1) (n + 2)) (by simp [reassoc_of% w]),
92	      (J.exact_succ n).comp_descToInjective _ _⟩
93	
94	/-- A morphism in `C` descends to a cochain map between injective resolutions. -/
95	def desc {Y Z : C} (f : Z ⟶ Y) (I : InjectiveResolution Y) (J : InjectiveResolution Z) :
96	    J.cocomplex ⟶ I.cocomplex :=
97	  CochainComplex.mkHom _ _ (descFZero f _ _) (descFOne f _ _) (descFOne_zero_comm f I J).symm
98	    fun n ⟨g, g', w⟩ => ⟨(descFSucc I J n g g' w.symm).1, (descFSucc I J n g g' w.symm).2.symm⟩
99	
100	/-- The resolution maps intertwine the descent of a morphism and that morphism. -/
101	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
102	theorem desc_commutes {Y Z : C} (f : Z ⟶ Y) (I : InjectiveResolution Y)
103	    (J : InjectiveResolution Z) : J.ι ≫ desc f I J = (CochainComplex.single₀ C).map f ≫ I.ι := by
104	  ext
105	  simp [desc, descFOne, descFZero]

109	    (I : InjectiveResolution Y) (J : InjectiveResolution Z) :
110	    J.ι.f 0 ≫ (desc f I J).f 0 = f ≫ I.ι.f 0 :=
111	  (HomologicalComplex.congr_hom (desc_commutes f I J) 0).trans (by simp)
112	
113	-- Now that we've checked this property of the descent, we can seal away the actual definition.

116	    (f : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (comm : I.ι ≫ f = 0) : I.cocomplex.X 1 ⟶ J.cocomplex.X 0 :=
117	  I.exact₀.descToInjective (f.f 0) (congr_fun (congr_arg HomologicalComplex.Hom.f comm) 0)

121	    (f : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (comm : I.ι ≫ f = 0) :
122	    I.cocomplex.d 0 1 ≫ descHomotopyZeroZero f comm = f.f 0 :=
123	  I.exact₀.comp_descToInjective _ _
124	
125	/-- An auxiliary definition for `descHomotopyZero`. -/
126	def descHomotopyZeroOne {Y Z : C} {I : InjectiveResolution Y} {J : InjectiveResolution Z}
127	    (f : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (comm : I.ι ≫ f = (0 : _ ⟶ J.cocomplex)) :

129	  (I.exact_succ 0).descToInjective (f.f 1 - descHomotopyZeroZero f comm ≫ J.cocomplex.d 0 1)
130	    (by rw [Preadditive.comp_sub, comp_descHomotopyZeroZero_assoc f comm,
131	          HomologicalComplex.Hom.comm, sub_self])

133	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
134	lemma comp_descHomotopyZeroOne {Y Z : C} {I : InjectiveResolution Y} {J : InjectiveResolution Z}
135	    (f : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (comm : I.ι ≫ f = (0 : _ ⟶ J.cocomplex)) :
136	    I.cocomplex.d 1 2 ≫ descHomotopyZeroOne f comm =
137	      f.f 1 - descHomotopyZeroZero f comm ≫ J.cocomplex.d 0 1 :=

148	      rw [Preadditive.comp_sub, ← HomologicalComplex.Hom.comm, w, Preadditive.add_comp,
149	        Category.assoc, Category.assoc, HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d, comp_zero,
150	        add_zero, sub_self])

154	    (f : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (n : ℕ) (g : I.cocomplex.X (n + 1) ⟶ J.cocomplex.X n)

161	/-- Any descent of the zero morphism is homotopic to zero. -/
162	def descHomotopyZero {Y Z : C} {I : InjectiveResolution Y} {J : InjectiveResolution Z}
163	    (f : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (comm : I.ι ≫ f = 0) : Homotopy f 0 :=
164	  Homotopy.mkCoinductive _ (descHomotopyZeroZero f comm) (by simp)
165	    (descHomotopyZeroOne f comm) (by simp) (fun n ⟨g, g', w⟩ =>
166	    ⟨descHomotopyZeroSucc f n g g' (by simp only [w, add_comm]), by simp⟩)
167	
168	/-- Two descents of the same morphism are homotopic. -/
169	def descHomotopy {Y Z : C} (f : Y ⟶ Z) {I : InjectiveResolution Y} {J : InjectiveResolution Z}
170	    (g h : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (g_comm : I.ι ≫ g = (CochainComplex.single₀ C).map f ≫ J.ι)
171	    (h_comm : I.ι ≫ h = (CochainComplex.single₀ C).map f ≫ J.ι) : Homotopy g h :=
172	  Homotopy.equivSubZero.invFun (descHomotopyZero _ (by simp [g_comm, h_comm]))
173	
174	/-- The descent of the identity morphism is homotopic to the identity cochain map. -/
175	def descIdHomotopy (X : C) (I : InjectiveResolution X) :
176	    Homotopy (desc (𝟙 X) I I) (𝟙 I.cocomplex) := by
177	  apply descHomotopy (𝟙 X) <;> simp
178	
179	/-- The descent of a composition is homotopic to the composition of the descents. -/
180	def descCompHomotopy {X Y Z : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (I : InjectiveResolution X)
181	    (J : InjectiveResolution Y) (K : InjectiveResolution Z) :
182	    Homotopy (desc (f ≫ g) K I) (desc f J I ≫ desc g K J) := by
183	  apply descHomotopy (f ≫ g) <;> simp
184	
185	-- We don't care about the actual definitions of these homotopies.
186	/-- Any two injective resolutions are homotopy equivalent. -/
187	def homotopyEquiv {X : C} (I J : InjectiveResolution X) :
188	    HomotopyEquiv I.cocomplex J.cocomplex where
189	  hom := desc (𝟙 X) J I
190	  inv := desc (𝟙 X) I J
191	  homotopyHomInvId := (descCompHomotopy (𝟙 X) (𝟙 X) I J I).symm.trans <| by
192	    simpa [id_comp] using descIdHomotopy _ _
193	  homotopyInvHomId := (descCompHomotopy (𝟙 X) (𝟙 X) J I J).symm.trans <| by
194	    simpa [id_comp] using descIdHomotopy _ _
195	
196	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
197	theorem homotopyEquiv_hom_ι {X : C} (I J : InjectiveResolution X) :
198	    I.ι ≫ (homotopyEquiv I J).hom = J.ι := by simp [homotopyEquiv]
199	
200	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
201	theorem homotopyEquiv_inv_ι {X : C} (I J : InjectiveResolution X) :
202	    J.ι ≫ (homotopyEquiv I J).inv = I.ι := by simp [homotopyEquiv]
203	
204	end Abelian

212	/-- An arbitrarily chosen injective resolution of an object. -/
213	abbrev injectiveResolution (Z : C) [HasInjectiveResolution Z] : InjectiveResolution Z :=

217	variable [HasInjectiveResolutions C]

219	/-- Taking injective resolutions is functorial,
220	if considered with target the homotopy category
221	(`ℕ`-indexed cochain complexes and cochain maps up to homotopy).
222	-/
223	def injectiveResolutions : C ⥤ HomotopyCategory C (ComplexShape.up ℕ) where
224	  obj X := (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).obj (injectiveResolution X).cocomplex
225	  map f := (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map (InjectiveResolution.desc f _ _)
226	  map_id X := by
227	    rw [← (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map_id]
228	    apply HomotopyCategory.eq_of_homotopy
229	    apply InjectiveResolution.descIdHomotopy
230	  map_comp f g := by
231	    rw [← (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map_comp]
232	    apply HomotopyCategory.eq_of_homotopy
233	    apply InjectiveResolution.descCompHomotopy

236	/-- If `I : InjectiveResolution X`, then the chosen `(injectiveResolutions C).obj X`
237	is isomorphic (in the homotopy category) to `I.cocomplex`. -/
238	def InjectiveResolution.iso {X : C} (I : InjectiveResolution X) :
239	    (injectiveResolutions C).obj X ≅
240	      (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).obj I.cocomplex :=
241	  HomotopyCategory.isoOfHomotopyEquiv (homotopyEquiv _ _)
242

244	@[reassoc]
245	lemma InjectiveResolution.iso_hom_naturality {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y)
246	    (I : InjectiveResolution X) (J : InjectiveResolution Y)
247	    (φ : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (comm : I.ι.f 0 ≫ φ.f 0 = f ≫ J.ι.f 0) :
248	    (injectiveResolutions C).map f ≫ J.iso.hom =
249	      I.iso.hom ≫ (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map φ := by
250	  apply HomotopyCategory.eq_of_homotopy
251	  apply descHomotopy f
252	  all_goals aesop
253	
254	@[reassoc]
255	lemma InjectiveResolution.iso_inv_naturality {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y)
256	    (I : InjectiveResolution X) (J : InjectiveResolution Y)
257	    (φ : I.cocomplex ⟶ J.cocomplex) (comm : I.ι.f 0 ≫ φ.f 0 = f ≫ J.ι.f 0) :
258	    I.iso.inv ≫ (injectiveResolutions C).map f =
259	      (HomotopyCategory.quotient _ _).map φ ≫ J.iso.inv := by
260	  rw [← cancel_mono (J.iso).hom, Category.assoc, iso_hom_naturality f I J φ comm,
261	    Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
262

269	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
270	theorem exact_f_d {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
271	    (ShortComplex.mk f (d f) (by simp)).Exact := by
272	  let α : ShortComplex.mk f (cokernel.π f) (by simp) ⟶ ShortComplex.mk f (d f) (by simp) :=
273	    { τ₁ := 𝟙 _
274	      τ₂ := 𝟙 _
275	      τ₃ := Injective.ι _ }
276	  rw [← ShortComplex.exact_iff_of_epi_of_isIso_of_mono α]
277	  apply ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel
278	  apply cokernelIsCokernel
279	
280	end
281	
282	namespace InjectiveResolution
283	
284	/-!
285	Our goal is to define `InjectiveResolution.of Z : InjectiveResolution Z`.
286	The `0`-th object in this resolution will just be `Injective.under Z`,
287	i.e. an arbitrarily chosen injective object with a map from `Z`.
288	After that, we build the `n+1`-st object as `Injective.syzygies`
289	applied to the previously constructed morphism,
290	and the map from the `n`-th object as `Injective.d`.
291	-/
292	
293	
294	variable [Abelian C] [EnoughInjectives C] (Z : C)
295	
296	-- The construction of the injective resolution `of` would be very, very slow
297	-- if it were not broken into separate definitions and lemmas
298	
299	/-- Auxiliary definition for `InjectiveResolution.of`. -/
300	def ofCocomplex : CochainComplex C ℕ :=
301	  CochainComplex.mk' (Injective.under Z) (Injective.syzygies (Injective.ι Z))
302	    (Injective.d (Injective.ι Z)) fun f => ⟨_, Injective.d f, by simp⟩
303	
304	lemma ofCocomplex_d_0_1 :
305	    (ofCocomplex Z).d 0 1 = d (Injective.ι Z) := by
306	  simp [ofCocomplex]
307	
308	lemma ofCocomplex_exactAt_succ (n : ℕ) :
309	    (ofCocomplex Z).ExactAt (n + 1) := by
310	  rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff' _ n (n + 1) (n + 1 + 1) (by simp) (by simp)]
311	  dsimp [ofCocomplex, CochainComplex.mk', CochainComplex.mk, HomologicalComplex.sc',
312	      HomologicalComplex.shortComplexFunctor']
313	  simp only [CochainComplex.of_d]
314	  match n with
315	  | 0 => apply exact_f_d ((CochainComplex.mkAux _ _ _
316	      (d (Injective.ι Z)) (d (d (Injective.ι Z))) _ _ 0).f)
317	  | n + 1 => apply exact_f_d ((CochainComplex.mkAux _ _ _
318	      (d (Injective.ι Z)) (d (d (Injective.ι Z))) _ _ (n + 1)).f)
319	
320	instance (n : ℕ) : Injective ((ofCocomplex Z).X n) := by
321	  obtain (_ | _ | _ | n) := n <;> apply Injective.injective_under
322	
323	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
324	/-- In any abelian category with enough injectives,
325	`InjectiveResolution.of Z` constructs an injective resolution of the object `Z`.
326	-/
327	irreducible_def of : InjectiveResolution Z where
328	  cocomplex := ofCocomplex Z
329	  ι := (CochainComplex.fromSingle₀Equiv _ _).symm ⟨Injective.ι Z,
330	    by rw [ofCocomplex_d_0_1, cokernel.condition_assoc, zero_comp]⟩
331	  quasiIso := ⟨fun n => by
332	    cases n
333	    · rw [CochainComplex.quasiIsoAt₀_iff, ShortComplex.quasiIso_iff_of_zeros]
334	      · refine (ShortComplex.exact_and_mono_f_iff_of_iso ?_).2
335	          ⟨exact_f_d (Injective.ι Z), by dsimp; infer_instance⟩
336	        exact ShortComplex.isoMk (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _) (by simp)
337	          (by simp [ofCocomplex])
338	      all_goals rfl
339	    · rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_exactAt]
340	      · apply ofCocomplex_exactAt_succ
341	      · apply CochainComplex.exactAt_succ_single_obj⟩
342	
343	instance (priority := 100) (Z : C) : HasInjectiveResolution Z where out := ⟨of Z⟩
344	
345	instance (priority := 100) : HasInjectiveResolutions C where out _ := inferInstance
346	
347	end InjectiveResolution
348	
349	variable [Abelian C]
350	
351	/-- Given an injective presentation `M → I`, the short complex `0 → M → I → N → 0`. -/
352	noncomputable abbrev InjectivePresentation.shortComplex
353	    {X : C} (ip : InjectivePresentation X) : ShortComplex C :=
354	  ShortComplex.mk ip.f (Limits.cokernel.π ip.f) (Limits.cokernel.condition ip.f)
355	
356	theorem InjectivePresentation.shortExact_shortComplex {X : C}
357	    (ip : InjectivePresentation X) : ip.shortComplex.ShortExact :=
358	  { exact := ShortComplex.exact_cokernel ip.f }
359	
360	end CategoryTheory

40	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [HasZeroObject C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
41	/--
42	An `InjectiveResolution Z` consists of a bundled `ℕ`-indexed cochain complex of injective objects,
43	along with a quasi-isomorphism from the complex consisting of just `Z` supported in degree `0`.
44	-/
45	structure InjectiveResolution (Z : C) where
46	  /-- the cochain complex involved in the resolution -/
47	  cocomplex : CochainComplex C ℕ
48	  /-- the cochain complex must be degreewise injective -/
49	  injective : ∀ n, Injective (cocomplex.X n) := by infer_instance
50	  /-- the cochain complex must have homology -/
51	  [hasHomology : ∀ i, cocomplex.HasHomology i]
52	  /-- the morphism from the single cochain complex with `Z` in degree `0` -/
53	  ι : (single₀ C).obj Z ⟶ cocomplex
54	  /-- the morphism from the single cochain complex with `Z` in degree `0` is a quasi-isomorphism -/
55	  quasiIso : QuasiIso ι := by infer_instance
56	
57	open InjectiveResolution in
58	attribute [instance] injective hasHomology InjectiveResolution.quasiIso
59	
60	/-- An object admits an injective resolution. -/
61	class HasInjectiveResolution (Z : C) : Prop where
62	  out : Nonempty (InjectiveResolution Z)
63	
64	attribute [inherit_doc HasInjectiveResolution] HasInjectiveResolution.out
65

71	`[EnoughInjectives C]` and `[Abelian C]`. -/
72	class HasInjectiveResolutions : Prop where
73	  out : ∀ Z : C, HasInjectiveResolution Z
74	
75	attribute [instance 100] HasInjectiveResolutions.out
76	
77	end

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — twisted biproduct of cochain complexes

This block builds the *structural* core of the dual Horseshoe Lemma as a general, injective-free
construction. Given two cochain complexes `K`, `L` and a degreewise family
`τ n : L.X n ⟶ K.X (n+1)` satisfying the cocycle identity
`L.d n (n+1) ≫ τ (n+1) = -(τ n ≫ K.d (n+1) (n+2))`, the biproduct objects `K.X n ⊞ L.X n` with the
twisted matrix differential `[[d_K, τ], [0, d_L]]` form a cochain complex `twistedBiprod τ hτ`,
the coprojection `K ⟶ twistedBiprod` and projection `twistedBiprod ⟶ L` are chain maps, and every
degree is the canonical split short exact sequence of the biproduct.

This is exactly the content of the blueprint sub-lemmas `lem:horseshoe_dComp` (the differential
squares to zero) and `lem:horseshoe_chainMap` (the coprojection/projection are chain maps and the
degrees split); it is isolated here free of injectivity because the only inputs are the cocycle
identity and the biproduct structure. The horseshoe specialises it to `K = I_A.cocomplex`,
`L = I_C.cocomplex` once the twist `τ` has been produced by injectivity. -/

section TwistedBiprod

variable {K L : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ} (τ : ∀ n, L.X n ⟶ K.X (n + 1))

/-- The twisted matrix differential `[[d_K, τ], [0, d_L]]` on the degreewise biproduct
`K.X n ⊞ L.X n ⟶ K.X (n+1) ⊞ L.X (n+1)`. -/
noncomputable def twistedBiprodD (n : ℕ) :
    (K.X n ⊞ L.X n) ⟶ (K.X (n + 1) ⊞ L.X (n + 1)) :=
  biprod.lift (biprod.fst ≫ K.d n (n + 1) + biprod.snd ≫ τ n) (biprod.snd ≫ L.d n (n + 1))

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma twistedBiprodD_fst (n : ℕ) :
    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ biprod.fst = biprod.fst ≫ K.d n (n + 1) + biprod.snd ≫ τ n := by
  simp [twistedBiprodD]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma twistedBiprodD_snd (n : ℕ) :
    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ biprod.snd = biprod.snd ≫ L.d n (n + 1) := by
  simp [twistedBiprodD]

variable (hτ : ∀ n, L.d n (n + 1) ≫ τ (n + 1) = -(τ n ≫ K.d (n + 1) (n + 2)))

include hτ in
lemma twistedBiprodD_comp (n : ℕ) :
    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ twistedBiprodD τ (n + 1) = 0 := by
  apply biprod.hom_ext
  · simp only [Category.assoc, twistedBiprodD_fst, Preadditive.comp_add,
      twistedBiprodD_fst_assoc, twistedBiprodD_snd_assoc, zero_comp]
    rw [Preadditive.add_comp, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d,
      comp_zero, zero_add, ← Preadditive.comp_add, hτ n, add_neg_cancel, comp_zero]
  · simp only [Category.assoc, twistedBiprodD_snd, twistedBiprodD_snd_assoc, zero_comp]
    rw [Category.assoc, HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d, comp_zero]

/-- The twisted biproduct cochain complex with differential `[[d_K, τ], [0, d_L]]`. -/
noncomputable def twistedBiprod : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ :=
  CochainComplex.of (fun n => K.X n ⊞ L.X n) (twistedBiprodD τ) (twistedBiprodD_comp τ hτ)

@[simp]
lemma twistedBiprod_X (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprod τ hτ).X n = (K.X n ⊞ L.X n) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma twistedBiprod_d (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprod τ hτ).d n (n + 1) = twistedBiprodD τ n :=
  CochainComplex.of_d _ _ _ _

/-- The coprojection `K ⟶ twistedBiprod τ hτ`, degreewise `biprod.inl`. -/
noncomputable def twistedBiprodInl : K ⟶ twistedBiprod τ hτ :=
  CochainComplex.ofHom _ _ _ _ _ _ (fun n => biprod.inl) (fun n => by
    simp only [twistedBiprodD_comp, twistedBiprod, CochainComplex.of_d]
    apply biprod.hom_ext <;> simp)

/-- The projection `twistedBiprod τ hτ ⟶ L`, degreewise `biprod.snd`. -/
noncomputable def twistedBiprodSnd : twistedBiprod τ hτ ⟶ L :=
  CochainComplex.ofHom _ _ _ _ _ _ (fun n => biprod.snd) (fun n => by
    simp only [twistedBiprodD_comp, twistedBiprod, CochainComplex.of_d, twistedBiprodD_snd])

@[simp]
lemma twistedBiprodInl_f (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprodInl τ hτ).f n = biprod.inl := rfl

@[simp]
lemma twistedBiprodSnd_f (n : ℕ) : (twistedBiprodSnd τ hτ).f n = biprod.snd := rfl

lemma twistedBiprodInl_comp_Snd : twistedBiprodInl τ hτ ≫ twistedBiprodSnd τ hτ = 0 := by
  ext n
  simp

/-- Each degree of `0 → K → twistedBiprod → L → 0` is the canonical split short exact sequence of
the biproduct `K.X n ⊞ L.X n`. -/
noncomputable def twistedBiprodSplitting (n : ℕ) :
    ((ShortComplex.mk _ _ (twistedBiprodInl_comp_Snd τ hτ)).map
      (HomologicalComplex.eval 𝒜 (ComplexShape.up ℕ) n)).Splitting where
  r := biprod.fst
  s := biprod.inr

end TwistedBiprod

section TwistedBiprod

variable {K L : CochainComplex 𝒜 ℕ} (τ : ∀ n, L.X n ⟶ K.X (n + 1))

/-- The twisted matrix differential `[[d_K, τ], [0, d_L]]` on the degreewise biproduct
`K.X n ⊞ L.X n ⟶ K.X (n+1) ⊞ L.X (n+1)`. -/
noncomputable def twistedBiprodD (n : ℕ) :
    (K.X n ⊞ L.X n) ⟶ (K.X (n + 1) ⊞ L.X (n + 1)) :=
  biprod.lift (biprod.fst ≫ K.d n (n + 1) + biprod.snd ≫ τ n) (biprod.snd ≫ L.d n (n + 1))

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma twistedBiprodD_fst (n : ℕ) :
    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ biprod.fst = biprod.fst ≫ K.d n (n + 1) + biprod.snd ≫ τ n := by
  simp [twistedBiprodD]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma twistedBiprodD_snd (n : ℕ) :
    twistedBiprodD τ n ≫ biprod.snd = biprod.snd ≫ L.d n (n + 1) := by

  simp

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the horseshoe twist family

Given a short exact sequence `ses : 0 → A → B → C → 0` and chosen injective resolutions
`I_A`, `I_C`, this block constructs the off-diagonal twist family
`τ n : I_C.X n ⟶ I_A.X (n+1)` together with the augmentation first component, satisfying the
cocycle identity `d_C n ≫ τ (n+1) = -(τ n ≫ d_A (n+1))`. Each `τ` is produced by the universal
lifting property of injectives (`Injective.factorThru` / `ShortComplex.Exact.descToInjective`)
against the cosyzygy monomorphisms of `I_C`. This is the blueprint sub-lemma `lem:horseshoe_twist`
(the recursion kernel). Combined with the `twistedBiprod` construction above it yields the middle
complex of the dual Horseshoe Lemma. -/

namespace InjectiveResolution

section OfShortExact

variable {ses : ShortComplex 𝒜} (hses : ses.ShortExact)
  (I_A : InjectiveResolution ses.X₁) (I_C : InjectiveResolution ses.X₃)

/-- First component `B ⟶ I_A^0` of the horseshoe augmentation: the injective extension of the
augmentation `A ⟶ I_A^0` along the monomorphism `A ↪ B`. -/
noncomputable def horseshoeβ₁ : ses.X₂ ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 0 :=
  haveI := hses.mono_f
  Injective.factorThru (I_A.ι.f 0) ses.f

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma f_comp_horseshoeβ₁ : ses.f ≫ horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A = I_A.ι.f 0 := by
  haveI := hses.mono_f
  exact Injective.comp_factorThru _ _

/-- Auxiliary map `C ⟶ I_A^1` through which `β₁ ≫ d_A^0` factors (since it kills `A`). -/
noncomputable def horseshoeH : ses.X₃ ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 1 :=
  hses.exact.descToInjective (horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 0 1) (by
    rw [← Category.assoc, f_comp_horseshoeβ₁, I_A.ι_f_zero_comp_complex_d])

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma g_comp_horseshoeH :
    ses.g ≫ horseshoeH hses I_A = horseshoeβ₁ hses I_A ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 0 1 :=
  hses.exact.comp_descToInjective _ _

lemma horseshoeH_comp_d : horseshoeH hses I_A ≫ I_A.cocomplex.d 1 2 = 0 := by
  haveI := hses.epi_g
  rw [← cancel_epi ses.g, comp_zero, ← Category.assoc, g_comp_horseshoeH, Category.assoc,
    HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d, comp_zero]

/-- The base twist `τ⁰ : I_C^0 ⟶ I_A^1`, extending `-(C ⟶ I_A^1)` along `C ↪ I_C^0`. -/
noncomputable def horseshoeτZero : I_C.cocomplex.X 0 ⟶ I_A.cocomplex.X 1 :=
  Injective.factorThru (-horseshoeH hses I_A) (I_C.ι.f 0)

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma ιC_comp_horseshoeτZero :
    I_C.ι.f 0 ≫ horseshoeτZero hses I_A I_C = -horseshoeH hses I_A :=
  Injective.comp_factorThru _ _

end OfShortExact

end InjectiveResolution
